slides.title: Learning Objectives
slides.content:
- Understand the core components of the scikit-learn library
- Become familiar with the scikit-learn workflow
- Implement a simple machine learning model
- Identify common pitfalls in using scikit-learn effectively

slides.title: Prerequisite Knowledge
slides.content:
- Basic understanding of Python programming
- Familiarity with fundamental machine learning concepts
- Experience with data manipulation in Python (using Pandas, Numpy)

slides.title: Conceptual Explanation
slides.content: Scikit-learn is a Python library for machine learning that features various tools for classification, regression, clustering, and more.

visual_assets.text: 8-Step Workflow: Data Collection, Data Preparation, Feature Selection, Model Training, Model Evaluation, Hyperparameter Tuning, Model Deployment, Model Monitoring
visual_assets.caption: Workflow Overview

slides.title: Scikit-learn Workflow
slides.content:
1. Data Collection
2. Data Preparation
3. Feature Selection
4. Model Training
5. Model Evaluation
6. Hyperparameter Tuning
7. Model Deployment
8. Model Monitoring

slides.title: Worked Example: Classification with Scikit-learn
slides.content: Let's classify the famous Iris dataset using a Decision Tree classifier.

visual_assets.code:
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris
from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier
X, y = load_iris(return_X_y=True)
clf = DecisionTreeClassifier().fit(X, y)
print(clf.predict(X[:2, :]))
visual_assets.caption: Predicting Iris Species

slides.title: Common Pitfalls
slides.content:
- Not scaling features
- Ignoring data imbalance
- Overfitting the model by not using cross-validation
- Misinterpreting model evaluation metrics

slides.title: Summary
slides.content:
- Scikit-learn simplifies machine learning implementations
- Follow the structured workflow for better model performance
- Always be cautious of common pitfalls to improve model accuracy and reliability
